question: Can the agent execute a multi-step task whose dependent sub-goals are each verified by a deterministic checkpoint, completing the WHOLE chain (one slip fails the task) at a rate, step-level success, and horizon length that clear the pre-registered thresholds?
constructs: deterministic-step-checkpoints (pure-function verifiers over agent output + threaded state; NOT an LLM judge)
horizon-length (longest fully-correct prefix; one slip ends the horizon)
completion-rate (fraction of tasks with every checkpoint passed)
primaryMetric: full-task completion rate (every dependent checkpoint passes, in order)
secondaryMetrics: mean horizon length (and as a fraction of task length)
primaryN: PENDING — example synthetic suite is 3 tasks / 12 steps (machinery only); a real run requires >= 100 tasks across >= 4 task families per agi-proof/preregistered-thresholds.md

verifierPurity.rule: every checkpoint is a deterministic pure function over (agent output, task state); zero LLM-judge calls, zero network, zero self-judging
verifierPurity.use: GO/NO-GO — a single non-deterministic or self-judged checkpoint voids the run

failClosed.rule: a dependent step whose prerequisite state was not recorded must fail (never pass on an unmet prerequisite)
failClosed.use: GO/NO-GO — guards against a step scoring correct without its dependency

horizonHonesty.rule: horizon length counts only the leading fully-correct prefix; steps after the first failure never count toward horizon

decontam.rule: task definitions and checkpoints are disjoint from any agent training data; synthetic examples are clearly labelled and excluded from training rows

stoppingRule: anytime-valid confidence sequence (tools/eval_stats.confidence_sequence_mean / confidence_sequence_from_summary) REQUIRED for any peeked metric; fixed-n bootstrap CI (tools/eval_stats.bootstrap_ci_paired) only at the pre-specified N
decontam: task prompts + gold checkpoints held disjoint from training (tools/assert_decontam.py); synthetic example suite is committed but excluded from training rows
ranking: guardrails first (verifier purity, fail-closed), then primary completion rate, then horizon length; step-level success is context, not the headline (a high step rate with low completion means the chain is not being completed)
honestLimits: deterministic checkpoints reward VERIFIABLE structure, not open-ended quality; they certify a chain was executed correctly, not that the task was solved wisely
the committed example suite is synthetic and tiny (3 tasks / 12 steps) — machinery proof only, NOT evidence; a cited number needs >= 100 tasks across >= 4 families and >= 3 seeds
horizon length on synthetic chains over-states real autonomy: real long-horizon tasks have ambiguous sub-goals a pure verifier cannot capture
this measures execution of a PRE-DEFINED chain; it does not measure the agent's ability to PLAN the chain (that is a separate, harder construct)

enforcement.harness: eval/long_horizon/ (tasks.py deterministic checkpoints + harness.py scoring) driven by tools/run_long_horizon_eval.py
enforcement.stats: tools/eval_stats.py — bootstrap CI (fixed-n) + anytime-valid confidence sequence (peeked) + mde_at_n power check
enforcement.tests: tests/test_long_horizon_eval.py — checkpoint logic, horizon-length, completion-rate + CI math, and the not-run/NO-GO pending artifact (deterministic mock agent)
enforcement.lint_claims: tools/lint_claims.py — no-overclaim copy must hold for any prose that cites this result
enforcement.decontam: tools/assert_decontam.py — task/checkpoint disjointness from training before any real run
enforcement.note: PRE-REGISTRATION ONLY. No real model run has been performed; the committed report (long-horizon-execution.PENDING.public-report.json) is status not_run / verdict NO-GO. Long-horizon execution is a Level-3+ gate (agi-proof/preregistered-thresholds.md) and is never auto-promoted.
